description: A type confusion vulnerability exists in V8's generic WebAssembly-to-JavaScript wrapper. The `WasmToJSObject` Torque macro in `src/builtins/js-to-wasm.tq` is responsible for converting wasm reference values into JavaScript objects when wasm calls an imported JS function. For the `anyref` (kAny) heap type, both in the nullable (kRefNull) and non-nullable (kRef) variants, the macro returns the wasm value as-is via `UnsafeCast<JSAny>(value)` without any runtime type checking. In WebAssembly's GC type system, `funcref` is a valid subtype of `anyref`, so a `WasmFuncRef` heap object (V8's internal representation of a wasm function reference) can legally appear as an anyref value at runtime. When wasm code calls an imported JavaScript function with an `anyref`-typed parameter and passes a funcref value, the WasmFuncRef escapes to JavaScript without being converted to a proper JSFunction via `WasmInternalFunction::GetOrCreateExternal`. JavaScript operations such as the `typeof` operator do not handle the `WASM_FUNC_REF_TYPE` instance type, causing V8 to abort with 'Unexpected instance type encountered'. The same logical flaw exists in the Turboshaft wrapper (`src/wasm/wrappers.cc`) and Turbofan wrapper (`src/compiler/wasm-compiler.cc`) `ToJS` functions, but the crash is triggered through the generic wrapper path (WasmToJsWrapperAsm -> WasmToJsWrapperCSA -> WasmToJSWrapper -> WasmToJSObject) which is active by default.

fixes.evidence_text: aa2cbd9e4ed70238694e07f1c525941aa1e04429 [wasm][wrapper] Don't store converted references in arguments
fixes.description: Local patch exported from https://chromium-review.googlesource.com/c/v8/v8/+/5804141 for reproducible fixed-image builds.
